items.title: The bull market faces higher likelihood of a Fed rate hike as Iran crisis intensifies

items.title: ServiceNow’s stock rises as earnings show momentum in cybersecurity
items.description: Against a gloomy backdrop for software sentiment, ServiceNow just topped revenue expectations.

items.title: IBM just cut its outlook, but not by as much as investors feared
items.description: IBM’s official earnings report comes after a bruising profit warning last week.

items.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?
items.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

items.title: I want to transfer $17,000 in credit-card debt. Why did Wells Fargo offer me only a $4,000 credit limit?
items.description: “I asked why the limit was so low, but they couldn’t give me an explanation.”

items.title: My second husband and I have kids from previous relationships. Should my house go to him or my children if I die first?
items.description: “We live in a house that I purchased entirely with money I had before we were married.”

items.title: Yes, the AI stock selloff looks terrifying. But it might actually save the bull market.
items.description: The hottest tech trades of 2026 just got slammed — smart investors are buying anyway.

items.title: The Treasury market touches a worrying milestone not seen since 2007
items.description: There are a host of challenges at work as the ‘long’ 30-year Treasury yield logs its longest stretch above 5% in 19 years

items.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
items.description: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”
